agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates driver route planning based on pending deliveries, with dynamic recalculation, customer notification, and vehicle-class safety constraints. It sits at the intersection of fleet management, delivery fulfillment, and customer experience.","bounded_context":"This feature spans at least four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning — owns route calculation, optimization algorithm, recalculation triggers, and stop-time estimates; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver profiles, shift lifecycle, vehicle classes, and licensing constraints; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, in-progress, failed, completed), zone assignment, and delivery-to-vehicle-class requirements; (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers and the 'next 3 stops' delivery window. Route Planning is the primary context; the others provide inputs via well-defined interfaces.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize some optimization objective (e.g., travel time, distance) given all pending deliveries in the driver's zone at time of calculation."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic region used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. A driver operates within exactly one zone per shift."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period during which a driver is active and eligible for route assignment. Begins when the driver clocks in and ends when they clock out."},{"term":"Recalculation Trigger","definition":"An event that invalidates the current optimized route and requires a new route to be computed. Defined triggers: new delivery added to zone, delivery marked as failed, driver manual deviation."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver physically departs from the sequence or path prescribed by their current optimized route."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"The set of the next three upcoming delivery stops on a driver's active route. Entering this window triggers customer notification."},{"term":"Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of a vehicle by its cargo capacity and type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight). Determines which deliveries a driver is eligible to fulfill."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"The vehicle class(es) a driver is certified and authorized to operate."},{"term":"Stop Time Estimate","definition":"The expected duration a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes system-wide; overridable per zone by the operations team."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that did not result in successful handoff to the customer, triggering route recalculation to remove or re-sequence the stop."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system MUST automatically compute and assign an optimized route covering all pending deliveries in the driver's zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A driver operating without an assigned route means deliveries are unserviced and the system's core value proposition is broken. Every active shift must have a route.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: within T seconds of shift start, driver.activeRoute is non-null AND contains all eligible pending deliveries in driver.zone"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A route MUST be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failing to incorporate new deliveries means they are silently dropped from fulfillment, violating delivery SLAs.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: after a new delivery is created in zone Z where driver D is active, driver D's route is recalculated AND the new delivery appears in the route"},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A route MUST be recalculated when a delivery on the route is marked as failed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A failed delivery that remains in the route wastes driver time and delays subsequent deliveries.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: after delivery X on driver D's route is marked FAILED, driver D's route is recalculated AND delivery X is removed or repositioned according to retry policy"},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A route MUST be recalculated when a driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Continuing to navigate a route that no longer matches the driver's actual position produces incorrect ETAs and misleading customer notifications.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when deviation is detected for driver D, a recalculation is triggered using driver D's actual current position as the new starting point"},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers MUST be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on an active route.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Customer notification is a core product promise; failing to notify erodes trust and may violate SLA commitments.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when delivery X transitions into the next-3-stops window, a notification is dispatched to delivery X's customer within N seconds"},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver MUST NOT be assigned a delivery that requires a vehicle class exceeding their licensed vehicle class.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a safety and regulatory constraint. A motorcycle driver physically cannot carry freight; violation could cause accidents, regulatory fines, and liability.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: for every delivery D in driver.activeRoute, D.requiredVehicleClass is compatible with driver.licensedVehicleClass"},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated stop time is 4 minutes per delivery stop.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational parameter, not a safety constraint. It is explicitly designed to be overridable by operations.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when no zone-level override exists, each stop's estimated time equals 4 minutes"},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"The operations team can override the estimated stop time on a per-zone basis.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an administrative policy lever, not a system invariant. The override mechanism itself is important but the specific values are operational decisions.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when ops sets zone Z stop time to T minutes, all route calculations for zone Z use T instead of the 4-minute default"}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The system assigns an optimized route containing all 5 deliveries and the driver's route status becomes ACTIVE"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 4 stops and a new delivery is added to their zone","when":"The new-delivery event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery (now 5 stops) and the driver receives the updated route"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery #3 is marked as failed","when":"The delivery-failed event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated without delivery #3 (or with it repositioned per retry policy) and the driver receives the updated route"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver deviates from the suggested route path","when":"The deviation is detected by the system","then":"The route is recalculated using the driver's current actual position as the origin"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer's delivery is currently stop #5 on a driver's route and stop #2 is completed","when":"The delivery advances to position #3 (entering the next-3-stops window)","then":"The customer receives a notification that their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle driver is active in zone A and a new freight delivery is added to zone A","when":"Route recalculation runs","then":"The freight delivery is NOT included in the motorcycle driver's route, regardless of optimization"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set zone B's stop time to 6 minutes","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone B","then":"Each stop's ETA uses 6 minutes, not the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A driver has an active route and a delivery enters the next-3-stops window due to recalculation (not sequential completion)","when":"The recalculated route places a delivery into the next-3-stops window","then":"The customer is notified, even though the transition was caused by recalculation rather than stop completion"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver starts their shift but there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The driver is assigned an empty route and enters an idle/waiting state until deliveries arrive"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Two drivers are active in the same zone","when":"A new delivery is added to the zone","then":"The delivery is assigned to exactly one driver's route based on optimization criteria, not duplicated across both"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Rapid-fire recalculation: three new deliveries added within seconds of each other","expected_behavior":"The system should debounce or batch recalculation triggers to avoid computing three separate routes in rapid succession. At minimum, the final route must include all three deliveries."},{"scenario":"Customer notification sent, then route recalculation moves their delivery OUT of the next-3-stops window","expected_behavior":"The system must handle the notification rollback — either send a revised notification ('your delivery has been delayed') or define that notifications are irrevocable once sent."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but then returns to the original route","expected_behavior":"Deviation detection should have a threshold — a temporary GPS drift or brief detour should not trigger constant recalculation. Once recalculated, returning to the old path does not revert to the old route."},{"scenario":"All deliveries in a zone require freight vehicles but only a motorcycle driver is active","expected_behavior":"No deliveries are assigned to the motorcycle driver. The system should flag unassignable deliveries to dispatch/operations for escalation."},{"scenario":"Operations overrides stop time to 0 minutes or a negative value","expected_behavior":"The system must validate the override value — stop time must be a positive duration with a sensible minimum bound."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends mid-route with remaining deliveries","expected_behavior":"Remaining deliveries must be returned to the pending pool for reassignment. Customers in the next-3-stops window who were already notified should receive an updated notification."},{"scenario":"A delivery is marked failed while it is currently the active stop (driver is at the location)","expected_behavior":"The delivery is marked failed, the route recalculates starting from the driver's current position, and the driver proceeds to the next stop."},{"scenario":"Concurrent recalculation triggers: new delivery added AND a delivery fails simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Both events must be incorporated into a single recalculation. The system must not lose either event due to race conditions."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, driver overtime, and failed delivery rates. Each percentage point improvement in route efficiency compounds across the entire fleet. Incorrect vehicle-class assignment could result in regulatory fines and insurance liability.","operational":"Dynamic recalculation increases system complexity and computational load. Debouncing strategy is critical — over-recalculation wastes compute resources; under-recalculation produces stale routes. The operations team needs a clear UI to manage zone-level stop-time overrides.","user":"Drivers get reduced cognitive load through automated routing. Customers get proactive delivery notifications, increasing satisfaction and reducing 'where is my package' support calls. However, frequent route changes could frustrate drivers if their current stop order keeps shifting.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Optimization algorithm correctness — a suboptimal algorithm silently degrades delivery throughput without obvious errors. (2) Vehicle-class constraint bypass — safety-critical rule that must never be circumvented. (3) Notification spam — recalculation-triggered notifications could bombard customers if not properly gated. (4) GPS/deviation detection false positives causing unnecessary recalculations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Core workflow changes — shifts from manual route planning to system-directed driving. Must trust the system's routing quality or adoption suffers."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proactive delivery notifications for the first time. Expectations are set by the 'next 3 stops' window — inaccurate ETAs will erode trust."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains new zone-level configuration lever (stop-time overrides). Needs visibility into unassignable deliveries and route recalculation frequency."},{"group":"Dispatch","impact":"Must handle edge cases where no eligible driver exists for a delivery (vehicle-class mismatch). Needs escalation workflow."},{"group":"Finance/Management","impact":"Expects measurable ROI from reduced fuel costs and improved delivery throughput. Needs reporting on route efficiency metrics."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec provides clear recalculation triggers, a firm vehicle-class constraint, and explicit notification rules. However, several key domain concepts are underspecified: the optimization objective is never stated, deviation detection mechanics are undefined, zone-to-driver assignment logic is unclear, and multi-driver zone behavior is unspecified. These ambiguities affect the core route calculation logic.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle-class constraint is unambiguous and safety-critical (BR-6)","Recalculation triggers are explicitly enumerated (BR-2, BR-3, BR-4)","Customer notification trigger at next-3-stops window is clear (BR-5)","Stop-time default and override mechanism is well-defined (BR-7, BR-8)"],"low_confidence_areas":["What happens to notifications already sent when recalculation moves a delivery out of the next-3-stops window","Shift-end handling for incomplete routes","How unassignable deliveries (no eligible driver) are escalated","Retry policy for failed deliveries — removed from route entirely or re-queued?"],"assumptions":["A driver operates in exactly one zone per shift","Vehicle class compatibility is a strict subset/hierarchy check, not a complex matrix","Route optimization runs synchronously at shift start (driver waits for route assignment)","The 'next 3 stops' window is recalculated after every route change, not just sequential completion"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The optimization objective for route calculation is never specified. 'Optimized route' could mean shortest total distance, fastest total time, maximum deliveries per hour, or a weighted combination. This fundamentally changes the algorithm and business outcomes.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Finance/Management"],"options":["Optimize for shortest travel time (fastest completion)","Optimize for shortest distance (lowest fuel cost)","Optimize for maximum on-time deliveries (SLA compliance)","Multi-objective optimization with configurable weights"],"recommendation":"Clarify with product team. Most logistics platforms optimize for shortest travel time as the default, with SLA-deadline constraints as hard boundaries. Recommend time-based optimization with delivery-deadline awareness."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation' detection is undefined. What constitutes a deviation — leaving a geofence corridor around the planned route? Missing an expected turn? Being more than X meters from the planned path for Y seconds? The threshold directly affects recalculation frequency and driver experience.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Distance-based: driver is > N meters from planned route for > T seconds","Sequence-based: driver skips the next planned stop and proceeds to a different one","Geofence-based: driver leaves a corridor polygon around the planned route segment"],"recommendation":"Recommend sequence-based detection (driver arrives at a stop that is not the next planned stop) as the primary trigger, supplemented by distance-based detection with a generous threshold (e.g., 500m for 2+ minutes) to avoid false positives from GPS drift or minor detours."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone behavior is unspecified. When multiple drivers are active in the same zone, how are pending deliveries partitioned among them? Is it first-come-first-served, load-balanced, proximity-based, or does each driver get an independent full optimization?","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Dispatch","Operations Team"],"options":["Global optimization: all drivers in a zone are optimized together, deliveries partitioned optimally","Independent assignment: each new delivery is assigned to the closest/least-loaded driver","Manual dispatch: operations team assigns deliveries to specific drivers, system only optimizes route order"],"recommendation":"Global optimization across all active drivers in a zone produces the best business outcomes but is computationally expensive. Recommend proximity-based assignment with load-balancing as a tiebreaker, with route-order optimization per driver."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Failed delivery handling is unclear — is a failed delivery removed from the route permanently, or re-queued for a later attempt? If re-queued, does it go back to the same driver's route or to the zone's pending pool? Most courier operations have a 2-3 attempt policy before returning to sender.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Remove permanently from this route, return to pending pool for reassignment","Re-queue to end of same driver's route for retry later in shift","Remove from route, escalate to operations for manual decision"],"recommendation":"Remove from the active route immediately (triggering recalculation per BR-3). Return to pending pool with a retry counter. If retry count < max attempts, it re-enters the zone's pending deliveries and may be picked up in the next recalculation cycle. Operations defines max retry attempts per zone."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates driver route planning based on pending deliveries, with dynamic recalculation, customer notification, and vehicle-class safety constraints. It sits at the intersection of fleet management, delivery fulfillment, and customer experience.","bounded_context":"This feature spans at least four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning — owns route calculation, optimization algorithm, recalculation trigger

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics domain scenario carefully.

This is a straightforward domain analysis request - no PR, no existing codebase. Let me break down the "Smart Route Optimization" feature.

Bounded contexts I can identify:
1. Route Optimization - route calculation, recalculation triggers
2. Fleet/Driver Management - vehicle classes, licensing, shift management
3. Delivery Management - pending deliveries, zones, delivery status
4. Customer Notification - the "next 3 stops" window notifications

Business rules to extract:


- Routes auto-assign when drivers start their shift, then recalculate when new deliveries arrive in their zone, when a delivery fails, or when a driver manually deviates from the route
- Customers get notified when they're within the next three stops on the route
- Motorcycles can't handle freight deliveries due to vehicle class constraints
- Each stop defaults to 4 minutes but operations can override this per zone

Now I'm thinking through the ambiguities here - zones aren't clearly defined in terms of how they're geographically bounded or assigned to drivers, the optimization criteria itself is vague (is it minimizing distance, time, or maximizing deliveries per route?), and the manual deviation trigger needs clarification on what constitutes a deviation and how the system detects it.

There's also the question of who manages the vehicle-to-delivery-class mappings, what happens when a delivery moves in and out of that "next 3 stops" notification window due to route recalculations, whether multiple recalculation triggers could cascade into excessive route changes, and what exactly counts as a failed delivery. Plus the per-zone stop time override doesn't account for delivery-type-specific variations.

The core issue is that this spec has multiple underspecified requirements - the vagueness around "optimized," "deviation," and "zone" creates genuine ambiguity rather than just missing details. My confidence is around 55% or lower because these interpretations could significantly change how the system behaves.

The threshold directly affects recalculation frequency and driver experience.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Distance-based: driver is > N meters from planned route for > T seconds","Sequence-based: driver skips the next planned stop and proceeds to a different one","Geofence-based: driver leaves a corridor polygon around the planned route segment"],"recommendation":"Recommend sequence-based detection (driver arrives at a stop that is not the next planned stop) as the primary trigger, supplemented by distance-based detection with a generous threshold (e.g., 500m for 2+ minutes) to avoid false positives from GPS drift or minor detours."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone behavior is unspecified. When multiple drivers are active in the same zone, how are pending deliveries partitioned among them? Is it first-come-first-served, load-balanced, proximity-based, or does each driver get an independent full optimization?","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Dispatch","Operations Team"],"options":["Global optimization: all drivers in a zone are optimized together, deliveries partitioned optimally","Independent assignment: each new delivery is assigned to the closest/least-loaded driver","Manual dispatch: operations team assigns deliveries to specific drivers, system only optimizes route order"],"recommendation":"Global optimization across all active drivers in a zone produces the best business outcomes but is computationally expensive. Recommend proximity-based assignment with load-balancing as a tiebreaker, with route-order optimization per driver."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Failed delivery handling is unclear — is a failed delivery removed from the route permanently, or re-queued for a later attempt? If re-queued, does it go back to the same driver's route or to the zone's pending pool? Most courier operations have a 2-3 attempt policy before returning to sender.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Remove permanently from this route, return to pending pool for reassignment","Re-queue to end of same driver's route for retry later in shift","Remove from route, escalate to operations for manual decision"],"recommendation":"Remove from the active route immediately (triggering recalculation per BR-3). Return to pending pool with a retry counter. If retry count < max attempts, it re-enters the zone's pending deliveries and may be picked up in the next recalculation cycle. Operations defines max retry attempts per zone."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates driver route planning based on pending deliveries, with dynamic recalculation, customer notification, and vehicle-class safety constraints. It sits at the intersection of fleet management, delivery fulfillment, and customer experience.","bounded_context":"This feature spans at least four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning — owns route calculation, optimization algorithm, recalculation triggers, and stop-time estimates; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver profiles, shift lifecycle, vehicle classes, and licensing constraints; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, in-progress, failed, completed), zone assignment, and delivery-to-vehicle-class requirements; (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers and the 'next 3 stops' delivery window. Route Planning is the primary context; the others provide inputs via well-defined interfaces.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize some optimization objective (e.g., travel time, distance) given all pending deliveries in the driver's zone at time of calculation."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic region used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. A driver operates within exactly one zone per shift."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period during which a driver is active and eligible for route assignment. Begins when the driver clocks in and ends when they clock out."},{"term":"Recalculation Trigger","definition":"An event that invalidates the current optimized route and requires a new route to be computed. Defined triggers: new delivery added to zone, delivery marked as failed, driver manual deviation."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver physically departs from the sequence or path prescribed by their current optimized route."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"The set of the next three upcoming delivery stops on a driver's active route. Entering this window triggers customer notification."},{"term":"Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of a vehicle by its cargo capacity and type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight). Determines which deliveries a driver is eligible to fulfill."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"The vehicle class(es) a driver is certified and authorized to operate."},{"term":"Stop Time Estimate","definition":"The expected duration a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes system-wide; overridable per zone by the operations team."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that did not result in successful handoff to the customer, triggering route recalculation to remove or re-sequence the stop."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system MUST automatically compute and assign an optimized route covering all pending deliveries in the driver's zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A driver operating without an assigned route means deliveries are unserviced and the system's core value proposition is broken. Every active shift must have a route.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: within T seconds of shift start, driver.activeRoute is non-null AND contains all eligible pending deliveries in driver.zone"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A route MUST be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failing to incorporate new deliveries means they are silently dropped from fulfillment, violating delivery SLAs.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: after a new delivery is created in zone Z where driver D is active, driver D's route is recalculated AND the new delivery appears in the route"},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A route MUST be recalculated when a delivery on the route is marked as failed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A failed delivery that remains in the route wastes driver time and delays subsequent deliveries.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: after delivery X on driver D's route is marked FAILED, driver D's route is recalculated AND delivery X is removed or repositioned according to retry policy"},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A route MUST be recalculated when a driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Continuing to navigate a route that no longer matches the driver's actual position produces incorrect ETAs and misleading customer notifications.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when deviation is detected for driver D, a recalculation is triggered using driver D's actual current position as the new starting point"},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers MUST be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on an active route.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Customer notification is a core product promise; failing to notify erodes trust and may violate SLA commitments.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when delivery X transitions into the next-3-stops window, a notification is dispatched to delivery X's customer within N seconds"},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver MUST NOT be assigned a delivery that requires a vehicle class exceeding their licensed vehicle class.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a safety and regulatory constraint. A motorcycle driver physically cannot carry freight; violation could cause accidents, regulatory fines, and liability.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: for every delivery D in driver.activeRoute, D.requiredVehicleClass is compatible with driver.licensedVehicleClass"},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated stop time is 4 minutes per delivery stop.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational parameter, not a safety constraint. It is explicitly designed to be overridable by operations.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when no zone-level override exists, each stop's estimated time equals 4 minutes"},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"The operations team can override the estimated stop time on a per-zone basis.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an administrative policy lever, not a system invariant. The override mechanism itself is important but the specific values are operational decisions.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when ops sets zone Z stop time to T minutes, all route calculations for zone Z use T instead of the 4-minute default"}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The system assigns an optimized route containing all 5 deliveries and the driver's route status becomes ACTIVE"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 4 stops and a new delivery is added to their zone","when":"The new-delivery event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery (now 5 stops) and the driver receives the updated route"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery #3 is marked as failed","when":"The delivery-failed event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated without delivery #3 (or with it repositioned per retry policy) and the driver receives the updated route"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver deviates from the suggested route path","when":"The deviation is detected by the system","then":"The route is recalculated using the driver's current actual position as the origin"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer's delivery is currently stop #5 on a driver's route and stop #2 is completed","when":"The delivery advances to position #3 (entering the next-3-stops window)","then":"The customer receives a notification that their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle driver is active in zone A and a new freight delivery is added to zone A","when":"Route recalculation runs","then":"The freight delivery is NOT included in the motorcycle driver's route, regardless of optimization"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set zone B's stop time to 6 minutes","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone B","then":"Each stop's ETA uses 6 minutes, not the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A driver has an active route and a delivery enters the next-3-stops window due to recalculation (not sequential completion)","when":"The recalculated route places a delivery into the next-3-stops window","then":"The customer is notified, even though the transition was caused by recalculation rather than stop completion"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver starts their shift but there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The driver is assigned an empty route and enters an idle/waiting state until deliveries arrive"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Two drivers are active in the same zone","when":"A new delivery is added to the zone","then":"The delivery is assigned to exactly one driver's route based on optimization criteria, not duplicated across both"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Rapid-fire recalculation: three new deliveries added within seconds of each other","expected_behavior":"The system should debounce or batch recalculation triggers to avoid computing three separate routes in rapid succession. At minimum, the final route must include all three deliveries."},{"scenario":"Customer notification sent, then route recalculation moves their delivery OUT of the next-3-stops window","expected_behavior":"The system must handle the notification rollback — either send a revised notification ('your delivery has been delayed') or define that notifications are irrevocable once sent."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but then returns to the original route","expected_behavior":"Deviation detection should have a threshold — a temporary GPS drift or brief detour should not trigger constant recalculation. Once recalculated, returning to the old path does not revert to the old route."},{"scenario":"All deliveries in a zone require freight vehicles but only a motorcycle driver is active","expected_behavior":"No deliveries are assigned to the motorcycle driver. The system should flag unassignable deliveries to dispatch/operations for escalation."},{"scenario":"Operations overrides stop time to 0 minutes or a negative value","expected_behavior":"The system must validate the override value — stop time must be a positive duration with a sensible minimum bound."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends mid-route with remaining deliveries","expected_behavior":"Remaining deliveries must be returned to the pending pool for reassignment. Customers in the next-3-stops window who were already notified should receive an updated notification."},{"scenario":"A delivery is marked failed while it is currently the active stop (driver is at the location)","expected_behavior":"The delivery is marked failed, the route recalculates starting from the driver's current position, and the driver proceeds to the next stop."},{"scenario":"Concurrent recalculation triggers: new delivery added AND a delivery fails simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Both events must be incorporated into a single recalculation. The system must not lose either event due to race conditions."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, driver overtime, and failed delivery rates. Each percentage point improvement in route efficiency compounds across the entire fleet. Incorrect vehicle-class assignment could result in regulatory fines and insurance liability.","operational":"Dynamic recalculation increases system complexity and computational load. Debouncing strategy is critical — over-recalculation wastes compute resources; under-recalculation produces stale routes. The operations team needs a clear UI to manage zone-level stop-time overrides.","user":"Drivers get reduced cognitive load through automated routing. Customers get proactive delivery notifications, increasing satisfaction and reducing 'where is my package' support calls. However, frequent route changes could frustrate drivers if their current stop order keeps shifting.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Optimization algorithm correctness — a suboptimal algorithm silently degrades delivery throughput without obvious errors. (2) Vehicle-class constraint bypass — safety-critical rule that must never be circumvented. (3) Notification spam — recalculation-triggered notifications could bombard customers if not properly gated. (4) GPS/deviation detection false positives causing unnecessary recalculations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Core workflow changes — shifts from manual route planning to system-directed driving. Must trust the system's routing quality or adoption suffers."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proactive delivery notifications for the first time. Expectations are set by the 'next 3 stops' window — inaccurate ETAs will erode trust."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains new zone-level configuration lever (stop-time overrides). Needs visibility into unassignable deliveries and route recalculation frequency."},{"group":"Dispatch","impact":"Must handle edge cases where no eligible driver exists for a delivery (vehicle-class mismatch). Needs escalation workflow."},{"group":"Finance/Management","impact":"Expects measurable ROI from reduced fuel costs and improved delivery throughput. Needs reporting on route efficiency metrics."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec provides clear recalculation triggers, a firm vehicle-class constraint, and explicit notification rules. However, several key domain concepts are underspecified: the optimization objective is never stated, deviation detection mechanics are undefined, zone-to-driver assignment logic is unclear, and multi-driver zone behavior is unspecified. These ambiguities affect the core route calculation logic.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle-class constraint is unambiguous and safety-critical (BR-6)","Recalculation triggers are explicitly enumerated (BR-2, BR-3, BR-4)","Customer notification trigger at next-3-stops window is clear (BR-5)","Stop-time default and override mechanism is well-defined (BR-7, BR-8)"],"low_confidence_areas":["What happens to notifications already sent when recalculation moves a delivery out of the next-3-stops window","Shift-end handling for incomplete routes","How unassignable deliveries (no eligible driver) are escalated","Retry policy for failed deliveries — removed from route entirely or re-queued?"],"assumptions":["A driver operates in exactly one zone per shift","Vehicle class compatibility is a strict subset/hierarchy check, not a complex matrix","Route optimization runs synchronously at shift start (driver waits for route assignment)","The 'next 3 stops' window is recalculated after every route change, not just sequential completion"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The optimization objective for route calculation is never specified. 'Optimized route' could mean shortest total distance, fastest total time, maximum deliveries per hour, or a weighted combination. This fundamentally changes the algorithm and business outcomes.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Finance/Management"],"options":["Optimize for shortest travel time (fastest completion)","Optimize for shortest distance (lowest fuel cost)","Optimize for maximum on-time deliveries (SLA compliance)","Multi-objective optimization with configurable weights"],"recommendation":"Clarify with product team. Most logistics platforms optimize for shortest travel time as the default, with SLA-deadline constraints as hard boundaries. Recommend time-based optimization with delivery-deadline awareness."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation' detection is undefined. What constitutes a deviation — leaving a geofence corridor around the planned route? Missing an expected turn? Being more than X meters from the planned path for Y seconds? The threshold directly affects recalculation frequency and driver experience.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Distance-based: driver is > N meters from planned route for > T seconds","Sequence-based: driver skips the next planned stop and proceeds to a different one","Geofence-based: driver leaves a corridor polygon around the planned route segment"],"recommendation":"Recommend sequence-based detection (driver arrives at a stop that is not the next planned stop) as the primary trigger, supplemented by distance-based detection with a generous threshold (e.g., 500m for 2+ minutes) to avoid false positives from GPS drift or minor detours."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone behavior is unspecified. When multiple drivers are active in the same zone, how are pending deliveries partitioned among them? Is it first-come-first-served, load-balanced, proximity-based, or does each driver get an independent full optimization?","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Dispatch","Operations Team"],"options":["Global optimization: all drivers in a zone are optimized together, deliveries partitioned optimally","Independent assignment: each new delivery is assigned to the closest/least-loaded driver","Manual dispatch: operations team assigns deliveries to specific drivers, system only optimizes route order"],"recommendation":"Global optimization across all active drivers in a zone produces the best business outcomes but is computationally expensive. Recommend proximity-based assignment with load-balancing as a tiebreaker, with route-order optimization per driver."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Failed delivery handling is unclear — is a failed delivery removed from the route permanently, or re-queued for a later attempt? If re-queued, does it go back to the same driver's route or to the zone's pending pool? Most courier operations have a 2-3 attempt policy before returning to sender.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Remove permanently from this route, return to pending pool for reassignment","Re-queue to end of same driver's route for retry later in shift","Remove from route, escalate to operations for manual decision"],"recommendation":"Remove from the active route immediately (triggering recalculation per BR-3). Return to pending pool with a retry counter. If retry count < max attempts, it re-enters the zone's pending deliveries and may be picked up in the next recalculation cycle. Operations defines max retry attempts per zone."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
